checks.description: The broker token is intentionally agent-global: it authorizes only the loopback broker, while session endpoints retain their own credentials.
Broker discovery is written as mode 0600 in a mode 0700 SDK directory.
The broker identity key is written as mode 0600 in a mode 0700 SDK directory.
The broker binds and advertises only 127.0.0.1.
A replacement broker receives a newly generated discovery token after its predecessor releases a stale lock.
Per-session endpoint tokens remain separate from the agent-global broker token and are not accepted as broker credentials.
Status output uses the redacted discovery representation and never emits the broker token.
No non-loopback broker URL is advertised, and a wrong-token WebSocket upgrade is rejected.
Discovery records, including PID and incarnation fields, are observation-only and must never authorize TERM or KILL cleanup; retained exact-child fixture leases are the sole test signal authority.
The hostile harness labels PID-zero liveness checks as observation-only and rejects their use as signal authority.
GSF1 and SSH1 frames, fd 3 control channels, and fixture timing controls are reachable only from explicitly test-labeled fixture launch paths, never production broker or session-host selection.
